title: Product Readiness / PAP Evaluation Checklist
summary: 0/30 product-readiness checks executed. This run is intentionally separate from the feat/twenty-aesthetic-clone tool sweep so broad product evals do not muddy the T01-T43 and PG01-PG07 tally.

global_rules.why_haiku_only: Managed Agent test runs must use Haiku only. Do not use Sonnet or Opus for this checklist.
global_rules.browser_tool: agent-browser or the in-app browser, using real UI interactions unless a check explicitly calls for a read-only DB/API inspection.
global_rules.test_credentials: Use local QA_USER_EMAIL / QA_USER_PASSWORD or the same test account used for the current QA run.
global_rules.external_actions: Use sandbox/test destinations only. For destructive or external-facing actions, first test rejection, then test approval only when the destination is safe.
global_rules.console_errors: Every UI check must capture browser console errors. Red console errors fail the item unless documented as pre-existing and unrelated.
global_rules.done_rule: Set done=true only when the UI behavior, side effect, and console/error checks have all been verified.

preflight.step: Start or verify the dev server
Log in through the browser UI
preflight.expected: Authenticated session lands on /chat without redirect loops.
Composer model selector shows Basic - Haiku 4.5 before any Managed Agent prompt is sent.
No red console errors on /chat, /crm/people, /automations, and /settings/agent/general.

preflight.step: Create or identify a unique QA run prefix
preflight.expected: All CRM records, files, triggers, tasks, and thread names created by this run include QA PAP 20260430.
Executor can find Langfuse traces or equivalent local trace/log evidence for each Managed Agent run.
preflight.notes: Mark blocked if Langfuse credentials are unavailable; do not block functional UI checks.

checks.name: Just-met lead capture creates CRM graph
checks.chat_prompt: I just met QA PAP 20260430 David Chen at a viewing for 22 Nassim Road. He's a buyer, budget $3.2M, wants a 3-bed with a balcony. His phone is 9182-7364 and email is qa-pap-david@example.test. He's relocating from Hong Kong in April. Save this in my CRM.
expected: Person/contact exists with name, email, phone, buyer context, budget, and relocation note.
Deal/opportunity exists for 22 Nassim Road with approximate value 3200000.
Contact and deal are linked.
Agent confirms the saved facts without inventing extra data.

side_effect_check.description: Verify contact/deal/link in CRM UI or read-only Supabase inspection.

checks.name: Follow-up email is drafted and gated
checks.chat_prompt: Draft a follow-up email to qa-pap-david@example.test thanking him for the 22 Nassim Road viewing. Mention the balcony and ask if he wants a second viewing this week. Send it if everything looks good.
expected: Approval card renders before any external send tool.
Click Reject first; no send_message or Composio send tool executes.
Repeat with a sandbox-safe destination and click Approve; exactly one send action executes.
Thread resumes cleanly after both reject and approve paths.

side_effect_check.description: Verify no send after rejection, one send after approval, and trace shows approval gating before external action.

checks.notes: Use a sandbox/test mailbox only for the approval path.

checks.name: Memory persists across threads
checks.chat_prompt: Remember that QA PAP 20260430 David Chen cares most about pet-friendly buildings and high floors. Then start a new thread and ask: what should I remember about David Chen?
expected: First thread writes the preference to the appropriate memory file.
Second thread recalls pet-friendly and high-floor preferences without being re-told.
Agent distinguishes durable memory from transient chat history.

side_effect_check.description: Read SOUL.md, USER.md, MEMORY.md, or memory/*.md via the app/tool and confirm the fact is present once.

checks.name: Memory correction does not duplicate stale facts
checks.chat_prompt: Correction: QA PAP 20260430 David Chen does not have a dog anymore. He still likes high floors, but pet-friendly is no longer important. Update what you remember.
expected: Memory is updated to remove or supersede pet-friendly preference.
A new thread reports the corrected preference, not both old and new facts.
Agent explains the correction succinctly.

side_effect_check.description: Inspect memory files for stale duplicate facts.

checks.name: Create, list, pause, resume, and delete scheduled automation
checks.chat_prompt: Create a QA PAP 20260430 automation that gives me a daily 9am briefing of overdue tasks and stale deals. Then show it in my automations list, pause it, resume it, and delete it.
expected: Automation appears in /automations after creation.
Pause and resume state changes are visible in UI.
Delete removes the automation or marks it inactive according to product behavior.
No duplicate active automation remains after cleanup.

side_effect_check.description: Verify agent_triggers row lifecycle and /automations UI state.

checks.name: Automation duplicate-run suppression
setup: Use a QA automation trigger from PAP05 or create a dedicated short-lived trigger.
Manually claim or fire the same due trigger twice if a safe local path exists.
expected: Only one active Managed Agent run starts for the automation.
Second claim skips or reports already running.
Parent automation/thread linkage is preserved.

side_effect_check.description: Inspect trigger execution rows/logs for one run and one skipped duplicate.

checks.notes: Mark blocked if there is no safe local way to fire the scheduler.

checks.name: CRM and file data are tenant-isolated
setup: Use two distinct test users/clients when available.
Create one QA PAP 20260430 CRM record and one storage file under client A.
expected: Client B cannot see client A CRM rows through UI, tools, or read-only SQL.
Client B cannot read client A storage files.
Agent refuses or reports no access instead of leaking data.

side_effect_check.description: Use user-authenticated reads only. Do not use service_role for the isolation verdict.

checks.notes: Mark blocked if only one test tenant is available.

checks.name: Approval event cannot be confirmed by the wrong tenant/session
setup: Create a pending approval for client A.
Attempt to confirm/reject it from client B or an unauthenticated context.
expected: Wrong tenant/session receives 401/403/not found.
Approval remains pending for client A.
External action does not execute.

side_effect_check.description: Inspect approval_events status and outbound action logs.

checks.notes: Also covers Telegram callback isolation if Telegram is configured.

checks.name: Stop button recovers composer and archives stalled run
checks.chat_prompt: Do a broad QA PAP 20260430 research pass: search the web, inspect my CRM, write a short memory note, and prepare a follow-up draft, but wait for my approval before any external send.
expected: Click Stop while the run is active.
Composer unlocks within a reasonable time.
Thread can send a new message after stop.
If the managed session remains running, archive escalation path runs and is visible in logs.

side_effect_check.description: Check chat UI state, /api/chat stop response, and session-runner logs.

checks.name: Refresh mid-run does not orphan the thread
checks.chat_prompt: Build me a detailed QA PAP 20260430 morning briefing from CRM tasks, deals, last interactions, and memory. Include stale follow-ups.
expected: Refresh the browser while the run is streaming.
Thread reloads with coherent run state.
No duplicate assistant messages or duplicate tool side effects appear.
Composer eventually returns to usable state.

side_effect_check.description: Compare message rows/tool result rows before and after refresh.

checks.name: Mobile chat and approval flow are usable
checks.chat_prompt: Draft a short follow-up to qa-pap-david@example.test and ask for approval before sending.
expected: Composer, model pill, approval card, tool cards, and message bubbles fit without horizontal overflow.
Approve and Reject buttons are tappable.
No text overlaps or clipped controls.

side_effect_check.description: Capture screenshot and browser console at mobile viewport.

checks.name: Trace captures prompt, tool calls, approvals, and terminal state
setup: Run any successful P0 chat workflow above.
expected: Trace contains thread/session/run identifiers.
Trace includes tool call names, arguments shape, success/failure result, and approval decision.
Trace terminal state matches UI terminal state.
Trace does not expose secrets, auth headers, or service role keys.

side_effect_check.description: Use Langfuse when available; otherwise use local structured logs.

checks.notes: Functional checks can pass even if this is blocked by missing observability credentials.

checks.name: Pipeline review finds stale opportunities
checks.chat_prompt: Show me every QA PAP 20260430 deal in negotiation or proposal stage. For each one, tell me the last interaction and whether it needs follow-up.
expected: Agent queries CRM rather than answering from memory only.
Deals are grouped by stage.
Stale deals are flagged based on last interaction date.
Agent says clearly when no matching deals exist.

side_effect_check.description: Compare response to current CRM rows/interactions.

checks.name: Client matching uses CRM preferences
checks.chat_prompt: I have a new QA PAP 20260430 3-bed listing at 22 Nassim Road for $3.2M with a balcony and city view. Which buyer contacts might be interested? Check budgets and preferences.
expected: Agent searches CRM contacts/deals.
Shortlist reasoning cites budget, bedroom count, location, balcony, or known preferences.
Agent does not invent preferences for contacts with no notes.

side_effect_check.description: Compare shortlist to seeded QA contacts.

checks.name: Morning briefing is concise and date-aware
checks.chat_prompt: Good morning. What QA PAP 20260430 tasks are due today or overdue? Any deals need attention?
expected: Agent uses the current date correctly.
Briefing separates due today, overdue, and stale deals.
Response is concise and does not fabricate work when no rows match.

side_effect_check.description: Compare to current crm_tasks/deals/interactions rows.

checks.name: Connections discovery and missing-permission path
checks.chat_prompt: What connected apps can you use for me right now? If email is not connected, tell me what is missing without trying to send anything.
expected: Agent lists connected/disconnected state accurately.
Missing email permission produces a clear next step.
No approval card or send action appears for read-only discovery.

side_effect_check.description: Compare chat answer to /settings/connections state.

checks.name: Connection lifecycle does not require action approval
setup: Use a safe OAuth provider/test account if available.
expected: Create/reauthorize/delete connection flows are clear in UI.
Connection readiness is not treated as an external content-send approval.
External actions through the connection still require approval.

side_effect_check.description: Verify connection state before and after lifecycle action.

checks.notes: Mark blocked if no sandbox provider account is available.

checks.name: Public web research feeds CRM-safe answer
checks.chat_prompt: Research the official website for 22 Nassim Road or a similar public listing source, summarize only facts you can verify, and do not update CRM unless I ask.
expected: Agent cites or names sources used.
Facts are clearly separated from assumptions.
No CRM mutation happens because the user did not ask to save.

side_effect_check.description: Verify no create/update tool calls after research-only prompt.

checks.name: Authenticated browser profile path is graceful
checks.chat_prompt: Open the saved browser profile for a login-gated property or CRM site and tell me whether you can access it. Do not make any changes.
expected: If profile exists, browser session opens and reports access state.
If profile is missing/expired, agent gives a clear reauth path.
No destructive browser action is performed.

side_effect_check.description: Watch embedded live browser state when available.

checks.notes: Mark blocked if Browser-Use Cloud or saved profile is unavailable.

checks.name: Agent asks for missing business judgment and resumes
checks.chat_prompt: I want to follow up with QA PAP 20260430 David Chen, but I am not sure whether to push for a second viewing or ask about financing. Ask me what direction to take, then draft the message.
expected: Inline question UI appears.
User reply resumes the same run/thread.
Final draft reflects the user's chosen direction.
No external send occurs without approval.

side_effect_check.description: Reply through the inline question UI, not by bypassing with direct API calls.

checks.name: Telegram pairing and approval callback
setup: Use a sandbox Telegram account/bot if configured.
expected: User can see pairing/readiness state.
Telegram approval notification maps to the correct pending approval.
Approve/Reject callback updates the web thread and does not double-execute.

side_effect_check.description: Verify approval_events and outbound action logs.

checks.notes: Mark blocked if Telegram env/webhook is not configured locally.

checks.name: Inline json-render specs render deterministically
checks.chat_prompt: Show me a compact dashboard for QA PAP 20260430 deals: count by stage, stale follow-ups, and open tasks. Use whatever visual view format is available.
expected: Agent emits valid spec fence when appropriate.
Frontend renders metrics/cards/charts without raw JSON leaking.
Invalid or partial specs do not crash the chat.

side_effect_check.description: Inspect rendered view on desktop and mobile.

checks.name: Long thread remains readable and scroll-stable
setup: Use a thread with at least 25 messages and multiple tool cards, or create one during this eval.
expected: Scroll up/down is smooth enough for use.
Tool cards retain readable colors.
No random characters, clipped message text, or horizontal overflow.
Mobile viewport remains usable.

side_effect_check.description: Capture desktop 1440px and mobile 390px screenshots.

checks.name: Fresh account becomes useful in under 10 minutes
setup: Use a fresh test user or reset setup state safely.
expected: Landing/login/onboarding route works.
SOUL.md, USER.md, MEMORY.md baseline files are created or recover gracefully.
Daily Orchestrator seeded automation appears in Automations.
User can send a useful first chat request within 10 minutes.

side_effect_check.description: Verify setup_progress, storage files, and agent_triggers state.

checks.notes: Mark blocked if no fresh account/reset path is available.

checks.name: Testing uses Haiku and helper models stay scoped
setup: Run any chat workflow that creates a title and later triggers compaction if practical.
expected: Managed Agent run uses claude-haiku-4-5 during QA.
Main agent session does not route through Vercel AI SDK or Gemini.
Gemini is used only for title generation or compaction helpers when those paths run.

side_effect_check.description: Inspect request logs, model labels, and trace metadata.

checks.name: Tool failure is visible and recoverable
checks.chat_prompt: Read a QA PAP 20260430 file that definitely does not exist, then continue by creating a todo reminding me to upload it later.
expected: Missing file error appears as a failed/readable tool result.
Agent continues to the follow-up todo rather than stalling.
Composer unlocks at terminal state.

side_effect_check.description: Verify todo exists and failed file read is visible.

checks.name: Server restart during/after run does not corrupt thread
setup: Start a non-destructive long-running chat task, then restart the local dev server.
expected: App reloads after server restart.
Thread does not lose persisted messages.
User can continue in the same or a clearly recovered state.
No duplicate side effects are created.

side_effect_check.description: Inspect message rows and side-effect rows for duplication.

checks.name: Common chat workflows feel usable on slow network
setup: Optionally use browser/network throttling or a known slow network.
checks.chat_prompt: Find QA PAP 20260430 David Chen, summarize his deal, and create one follow-up task for tomorrow.
expected: Initial response/tool cards begin streaming without a long blank wait.
Tool completion cards render progressively.
No artificial short timeout fails normal Supabase/Anthropic calls.
Final state is correct even when slow.

side_effect_check.description: Record time to first visible progress and total terminal time.

checks.name: Health, headers, and rate-limit smoke
expected: /api/health returns 200 with healthy or documented degraded checks.
Security headers are present on page responses.
Rate limiting fails open or returns clear retry behavior according to current product design.
No secrets appear in errors or response bodies.

side_effect_check.description: Follow docs/qa/30-production-hardening.md for exact curl checks.

checks.name: QA data cleanup is complete
expected: All QA PAP 20260430 CRM records/tasks/deals/interactions are deleted or clearly marked test data.
All QA PAP 20260430 triggers are paused/deleted.
All QA PAP 20260430 storage files are removed unless intentionally retained as evidence.
No pending approvals or external draft sends remain.

side_effect_check.description: Search by QA PAP 20260430 prefix across CRM, triggers, tasks, storage, and approvals.

post_run.tally_command: Count checks where result is pass, pass-with-note, fail, or blocked. Include preflight separately.
deliverables: Filled-in checklist.json with done/result/notes populated for every preflight and check entry.
One repro markdown per fail or serious pass-with-note under /tmp/sunder-qa/product-readiness-pap/issues/<ID>-<slug>.md.
Short summary at the top of this JSON: '<N>/30 product checks pass, <M> pass-with-note, <F> fail, <B> blocked'.
exit_criteria: Every P0 item is pass or explicitly blocked for an environment reason.
All external actions have approval evidence.
All created QA test data is cleaned up or intentionally retained with notes.
